headline: Putrajaya's RM1.4 billion smart city project has zero measurable outcomes
context: The Putrajaya Smart City Initiative launched in 2021 with RM1.4 billion allocation has produced no published outcome metrics, KPIs, or measurable improvements after four years.

cards.big: RM1.4 billion smart city project — 4 years later, zero measurable results published
cards.sub: Putrajaya initiative spent billions without defining what success looks like

cards.big: Project approval did not include mandatory KPI framework or outcome metrics
cards.sub: Cabinet approved spending without requiring any measurable accountability targets

cards.big: RM1.4 billion equals the annual development budget of Kelantan and Perlis combined
cards.sub: Opportunity cost of unaccountable spending falls on states that need investment most

cards.big: Seoul's smart city programme publishes quarterly dashboards with 42 measurable indicators
cards.sub: International best practice demonstrates outcome measurement is feasible and expected

cards.big: A project that spends RM1.4 billion without measuring anything isn't smart — it's just expensive
Should all mega-projects above RM500 million require published quarterly outcome metrics?
